nodes.label: Load and apply a LKG file that fails checksum or generation validation rather than rejecting it and retaining the prior valid state.
Accept a delete-approval marker with a generation that does not match the current key generation, or one signed by a non-leader node.
Accept a write to a registry-managed critical key from a writer_leader_id or service that does not match the registered owner.

Add an entry to causal_rules.yaml based on theoretical reasoning without
a corresponding incident (INC-YYYY-NNNN) that demonstrated the failure chain.
Hypothetical rules clutter the causal graph with unverifiable noise.

When the doctor flags ops_knowledge.seed_drift, have a human operator
overwrite the seed-tagged entry directly so the finding stops firing,
without investigating why ai-memory diverged from the bundle.

An operator publishes a test build with publisher=core@globular.io
and channel=DEV to avoid changing the publisher. This pollutes the
official namespace with a non-authoritative build and could be confused
with a real official artifact by resolution logic.
Allow a reconcile tick to prevent or delay the terminal commit of an already-running reconcile phase by acquiring shared resources first.
Text-based annotation scanner flags string literal content as real annotations
Apply a topology drift action without checking the topology generation, allowing stale drift plans to modify live topology.

Directly modify path_weights.yaml without going through the learn_from_fix
proposal workflow and without human review. Weight changes alter the ranking
of all decision paths for every future preflight call.

A reconciler reads installed_state (Layer 3) and treats "installed"
as proof that the service is currently serving traffic. The
unit may be inactive (oom-killed, crashloop, never started). DNS
records computed from installed_state alone produce ghost records
for every unit that ever installed but isn't running right now.
Treat the presence of an installed-state record in etcd as proof that the corresponding service is currently running and healthy.
Assume that because a service artifact is published (AVAILABLE in the repository), its DesiredService record already exists in etcd and nodes will install it automatically.

During cross-cluster sync, Cluster B's local build (local@cluster-b,
storage@1.2.43+local.cluster-b.1) is imported and the sync logic
promotes it to official stable to simplify the merge. The receiving
cluster's official desired state now points to non-official bytes.
Automatically lower Scylla keyspace RF below policy when storage nodes decrease. RF must only ever increase automatically, never decrease.

Automatically install an older package version when the current version crashes
or fails its health check, without explicit operator approval.
Annotation scanner validates production Go source only
Awareness audit warnings must be triaged, not silently accumulated
A node may auto-install a missing awareness bundle only when the candidate matches the active release-index and passes integrity verification
Every awareness bundle must record the source git SHA used to generate it
awareness impact-ci must exit 1 when required tests are missing for changed files
globular awareness build must use --clean after YAML knowledge edits to prevent stale edge accumulation
Awareness source-availability sentinel is collapsed into a critical-severity missing-evidence finding
Every promoted failure mode must link to a regression test or explicitly declare why no test exists
Every edge kind must be classified into a domain (decision/information/proof/risk/proposal)
Every awareness graph edge must have a traceable provenance
ExplainImpactByFile must return graph path traces and missing-link suggestions
Causal rules must be derived from observed incidents, not hypothetical chains
Editing awareness YAML knowledge files requires a graph rebuild before the decision context is trustworthy
A promoted incident must be traceable from incident → failure_mode → invariant or forbidden_fix → test → future detection
awareness.decision_context must never return bare NO_MATCH — always include coverage and blind_spots
MCP must not expose promote_proposal as a tool
Awareness must not be silent for sensitive architectural tasks
A NO_MATCH awareness result must never be interpreted as evidence that a change is safe
Node context results must not flood with irrelevant matches
awareness.preflight or awareness.decision_context must run before editing any high-risk awareness file
Semantic-diff authority movement across Repository/Desired/Installed/Runtime layers must require explicit review and strong awareness coverage
Semantic paths between awareness nodes must include a human-readable explanation
Awareness source-scanning tools must use a verified repo root or report unverified — never silently fall back to a wrong tree
A stale bundle may provide hints but must not produce a safety verdict
Path weight changes must be proposed via learn_from_fix and require explicit human approval before promotion

Create a causal_rules.yaml entry retroactively, from memory or logs, without
the actual symptom keywords that were observed during the incident. Without
real symptom keywords, the rule can never match a future incident's log output.
Retry the same install action on every reconcile tick without classifying whether the failure is transient or deterministic.
Refuse to publish authoritative state because a cache, projection, or secondary index is unavailable. Authority must publish regardless of derived-state health.
Allow a slow or unresponsive external backend (Scylla, DNS, etcd) to block the main controller reconcile loop indefinitely without a timeout gate.
/var/lib/globular/bootstrap.enabled flag found on a node after day-1 join completed — security regression
Change the MCP server's listen port from 10260 to a different port to avoid the orphan-held port conflict, leaving the orphaned process alive and the original port occupied.

A local build fails the official namespace seal because its digest
differs from the published artifact. Fix attempt: update the stored
digest in the ledger to match the incoming local bytes.
This destroys official immutability and lets any cluster silently
corrupt the official stable identity without detection.
Implement a reconciliation circuit breaker that halts the entire reconcile loop when any single service install fails, rather than isolating that service and continuing with others.
Mark the cluster as converged or allow node-agent to install from bootstrap records before the controller promotion reconciler has validated and rewritten them as authoritative.
Delete or clear the in-flight install action from the action queue before the installed-state and result records are written and confirmed in etcd.
Delete or clear an action record before the installed-state and result promotion are committed to etcd in a single transaction.
objectstore-apply-watcher exits its goroutine when etcd watch returns 'mvcc compacted'; not re-established

Treat "no source tree available to scan" — whether the cause is an
empty repoRoot, a missing bundle, an EPERM on a runtime dir, or a
non-git cwd — as if it were a critical-severity missing-evidence
finding (e.g. REQUIRED_TEST_MISSING, "0 Go files indexed critical",
"N gaps have missing tests"). It conflates "we couldn't check" with
"we checked and found nothing," and produces a wall of false alarms
every time awareness runs on a host without source — which is every
production MCP host. Three separate incidents in 2026-05 (62 fake
missing tests, 39 fake missing fix_case tests, 0% coverage critical)
all come from this shape.

Killing controller processes does not clear lease contention — the killed process
held the lease, and killing it without etcd being healthy means the lease expires
and all remaining controllers immediately attempt re-election, adding more write
pressure. Only etcd stability resolves lease contention.
controller leader lease expired because etcd cannot commit writes during NOSPACE or leader instability

Restarting controller processes while etcd is in NOSPACE or leader-instability state
causes each restarted instance to immediately attempt to write a new leader lease to
etcd. These writes all fail (NOSPACE) or contend during re-election, amplifying the
load on an already-stressed etcd cluster and delaying recovery.
No infinite deterministic retry

In a switch over verification status, let the `default` branch
capture both genuinely-missing tests AND
"couldn't-check"/"unverified"/"no-tests-required" entries, so all
of them get reported under one bucket. The operator sees "N gaps
have missing tests" when the actual condition is "we have no repo
source to scan."
Critical control-plane queries must be bounded
Unbounded query blocks critical reconcile loop
Missing control-plane key is not a stop or delete command
Missing etcd key causes runtime shutdown
Critical desired-state key deleted without approval
Desired-state deletion requires explicit audited intent
Every critical key must have one authoritative owner
Critical etcd key has no registered owner or policy
A node must not reach workload_ready without a verified awareness bundle matching the active release

Add a new edge kind with an optimistic "decision" domain classification before
the edge has been used in real decision paths and verified to carry actionable
guidance. Overly broad decision classification inflates path scores and erodes
trust in decision_context output.
Delete a critical etcd key without checking the registry-defined delete policy. Keys with DeleteNeverAutomatic must require explicit operator approval.

When the controller install-storms against a build_id that the
repository can no longer resolve ("build_id X not found for
name=Y"), the operator deletes the ServiceDesiredVersion /
InfrastructureRelease row pinning build_id X to "clear the error".
The install loop stops, but desired state is now inconsistent
with the BOM and the orphaning continues silently — every later
cluster operation reads desired state and sees the wrong version.
Delete a critical desired-state key from etcd without first creating the delete-approval marker at /globular/<domain>/delete_approval/<generation>.
Delete or reset runtime data when the system is in an ambiguous state (recovering, no quorum, missing desired-state key) without explicit operator approval.
Interpret an etcd read timeout as a stop or delete intent and halt the runtime service. Timeout must be treated as a temporary authority unavailability, not a destructive command.
Derive awareness bundle build_id from the tarball filename token (short/hash-like suffix) and pass it to install verification as if it were the authoritative UUID build_id.
Derived state failure must not block authority publication
Projection failure prevents authoritative state publication
Bootstrap state treated as authoritative desired state
Bootstrap desired state requires explicit promotion
Desired build_id must not mutate after resolution
Ambiguous or malformed disable spec stops runtime
Destructive actions require explicit validated guard
Deterministic install failure causes infinite retry loop
Dispatch a workflow when the Scylla backend is known to be unavailable or degraded, producing orphaned workflow records.
DNS records point to nodes where service is planned, inactive, or not installed
A DNS record MUST point only at nodes where the promised service is installed AND runtime-healthy
Wildcard (*.<domain>) and api.<domain> records MUST be gated identically to gateway.<domain>

Edit any awareness YAML knowledge file (invariants.yaml, forbidden_fixes.yaml,
causal_rules.yaml, failure_modes.yaml, agent_playbooks.yaml, path_weights.yaml)
and then immediately rely on the graph's decision_context output without rebuilding.
The graph is now stale and will return paths that do not reflect the edit.

Modify the impact path traversal algorithm (TraverseImpactPaths, traverseTyped,
edgeTrust, or impactEdgeKinds) without updating or adding tests that verify the
scoring behavior of the result. Traversal changes silently alter which paths the
decision_context returns.

Run etcdctl alarm disarm before the etcd disk is actually below quota — specifically,
before compact + defrag + verified disk size reduction have all completed.
Every Globular service must be able to reach its configured etcd endpoint at startup; no silent fallback
etcd leader instability: repeated leader loss caused by NOSPACE or disk I/O pressure
etcd NOSPACE alarm: database space exceeded — all writes rejected, control-plane cascade begins

Restarting etcd nodes when NOSPACE is active does not help. The restarted node
rejoins the cluster but immediately encounters the same alarm condition and cannot
commit writes. Leader election continues to fail. Resolve the disk pressure first.
Missing evidence must not be treated as evidence of failure unless the contract explicitly says absence proves failure
MCP service orphaned processes hold port 10260, blocking WaitActive and install convergence

When `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` fails, fall back to
`os.Getwd()` and return the cwd as if it were the repo root. On a
production MCP host the cwd is the install dir (no source files),
which makes every subsequent scan return zero matches and produce
critical-severity REQUIRED_TEST_MISSING / 0% coverage findings.
Archive extraction must reject entries that escape the destination directory
Client-streaming file writes must resolve and authorize the destination path before any storage write
File service runtime identity must match proto service name, registry name, and TLS certificate SANs
Delete operations must reject empty path, root path, parent traversal, or malformed absolute paths
All file paths must be normalized and constrained to approved virtual roots before storage access
Public directory routing must use the cluster public directory authority, not local inference
URL-based file upload must restrict protocols and block private/metadata network targets

Mutate node.Identity.Ips to remove the cluster VIP before publishing the
identity, so downstream code that reads Ips[0] never sees the VIP.
Destroys the multi-IP truth (NodeIdentity.Ips is supposed to be the full
list) to paper over consumers that picked the wrong helper.
Partial objectstore snapshot classifies absent nodes as known-down, firing false quorum-loss findings
keepalived VIP transition causes healthy etcd member eviction and control-plane instability
A Virtual IP, load balancer address, or failover endpoint is used as the stable identity of a node or cluster member
A collector or inventory gap is interpreted as proof that a node, service, or dependency is down
UI or CLI reports success immediately after dispatching an irreversible or destructive action
A global healthy/green status conceals a degraded or unknown subsystem
Permission errors displayed as generic network or unknown errors
Two processes or nodes simultaneously believe they are the authority for the same resource
A cached or locally computed state is served in place of authoritative live state after the TTL expires

Use AI-generated or LLM-drafted text to describe a system state that the
backend did not confirm — including invented success, invented failures,
invented approvals, or speculation presented as observation.

Report success to the user immediately after dispatching an async,
irreversible, or destructive action — before the workflow or backend
has confirmed terminal success.

Replace a cluster DNS name, node IP, or VIP with localhost or 127.0.0.1
to work around a TLS, DNS, SAN, or routing failure.

Treat missing inventory, absent collector data, or an absent NodeRecord
in a snapshot as evidence that a node, service, or member is known-down.

Report success to the user for a destructive or irreversible operation
before backend or workflow confirmation is received.

Fire a quorum-loss finding or emit a KNOWN_DOWN classification for a node
that is absent from an incomplete objectstore snapshot, treating absence
as evidence of failure rather than a collector gap.

Display a gRPC PERMISSION_DENIED error as a generic network error,
connection failure, or unknown error, causing the user to diagnose
connectivity instead of authorization.

Infer runtime health or liveness from desired state, desired-release records,
or install-workflow completions instead of a live runtime probe.

Enable a destructive or state-mutating UI action button without first
checking the user's RBAC permission for that action.

Render an unknown or unobserved state as healthy/green in status rollups,
badges, or CLI output.

Use a floating Virtual IP, load balancer address, keepalived VIP, or
failover endpoint as the stable identity of a node in etcd, ScyllaDB,
MinIO, or any other membership/leadership system.

Assume a node holds the cluster VIP because it has the 'gateway' profile,
because keepalived is installed, or because desired topology includes it
as a gateway node — without reading the live VRRP state from etcd.
First 3 nodes of any cluster MUST have profiles core+control-plane+storage to satisfy etcd / ScyllaDB / MinIO quorum
Refuse to serve artifact listing, manifest queries, or version resolution when MinIO is unavailable, even though these operations can be satisfied from ScyllaDB alone.
Keep the hardcoded commandPackage() list in the doctor rule instead of migrating to catalog-driven package kind checks. The list will drift as new command packages are added.

Maintain a hardcoded list of service names or prefixes inside the reconciler to
classify packages as infrastructure vs service kind, duplicating the canonical
registry.

Maintain a hardcoded map of node-id → MinIO pool / etcd peer / DNS record
in the controller so the VIP-contaminated identity comparison can be
skipped. Replaces a structural fix with a per-cluster table the operator
must keep in sync.
Stable node identity must not be derived from a floating VIP or failover reachability address

Use IsInstallableByPin (publish_state only) as the sole filter in
buildIDIndexFromManifests, allowing split-state build_ids to appear as
"resolvable" in RepositoryBuildIDIndex even though the resolver would reject them.
The doctor then stays silent on a desired pin that will generate an endless
DesiredBuildIdOrphaned error for every node that tries to install it.

Remove or bypass the artifact_state check in resolveByBuildID / isRowInstallable
so that a row with publish_state=PUBLISHED is always treated as installable regardless
of artifact_state. This "fixes" the DesiredBuildIdOrphaned symptom by hiding the
split-state signal rather than repairing the underlying data inconsistency.
Skip the backend health preflight check when dispatching workflows, relying on Scylla to surface errors during the write rather than failing fast before creating the record.
Ignore or suppress the ctx.Err() result from a bounded query in a critical path, allowing the caller to proceed as if the query succeeded.

Score impact paths without giving extra weight to edges that lead to
forbidden_fix nodes. Paths that terminate at forbidden_fix nodes are PRE-EDIT
decision paths — the most actionable signals in the graph. Scoring them the
same as information paths causes them to be buried under less relevant results.
Fail or use empty config when the authority source is unreachable instead of falling back to the validated LKG file.
Use only the package name (not the catalog-defined kind) to decide whether a systemd unit is required. This causes false positives for new command packages.
Allow a critical keyspace to remain below required RF on a multi-node cluster without emitting a doctor finding or repair action.

collectDesiredBuildIDs scans only Spec.BuildID and forgets that
Status.ResolvedBuildID is also a live pin (the controller writes
it when resolving a version policy at dispatch time). The guard
blocks deletion of the SPEC build_id but happily archives the
RESOLVED one, breaking installs mid-rollout.
Assume systemd's Restart= directive cleanly terminates all child processes from a prior cgroup escape when the service restarts, without adding an ExecStartPre kill step.

Widen the drift-detection tolerance so "small" content differences between
the bundle and ai-memory no longer trigger the finding. Reduces noise by
losing signal that the immutability layer might be broken.

Infer package kind (infrastructure vs service) from the service name string (e.g.
"xds" implies infrastructure) rather than reading from the canonical package
metadata registry.
Infer that a node should run MinIO because its data directory exists or because MinIO was previously running, rather than reading the authoritative ObjectStoreDesiredState from etcd.
Infer MinIO pool membership from local disk (data directory, .minio.sys, last-run topology) when /globular/objectstore/config is absent.
Decide topology membership or configuration from local disk state (minio data directory, scylla data files) instead of the authoritative desired-state in etcd.
Infrastructure desired_hash must be computed consistently
Heartbeat is observer-only — it must never set desired state
Service never installs on any node because ServiceDesiredVersion record is absent
Install result must commit atomically
Leader dies during install result promotion
Keep service drift finding at WARN severity indefinitely regardless of age or affected services. Drift older than 5 minutes is ERROR; drift on critical services is CRITICAL.

Add `if os.Getenv("XYZ_ENDPOINT") != "" { return env }` "for dev only" so
local builds don't need etcd at startup. Ships per-node mutable
configuration into the same binary as production code paths and breaks
the etcd-is-sole-source-of-truth rule.
keepalived MUST only advertise as MASTER when local services are healthy; failover triggered by health check failure

A node's records keep pointing at a half-dead service, so the
operator lowers the TTL from 60s to 5s to "shorten the bad
window." Caching layers (resolver, OS, gRPC client connection
pool) still cache the bad answer for longer than the TTL; the
real bug — publishing the record at all — is unfixed; cluster-
wide query load multiplies.

Reduce the score penalty for paths with TrustInvalid edges (currently -100)
to make them "less invisible" in the output. Invalid paths represent broken
or stale graph edges — making them visible in decision output is more
dangerous than filtering them.
Manually set ServiceRelease.Phase to AVAILABLE in etcd after a wave succeeds but the commit RPC fails, bypassing the workflow's terminal-state commit.
Call the ServicesDesiredSet RPC individually for each service to work around a missing or incomplete BOM, creating desired-state records outside the platform-upgrade pipeline.
Manually write DesiredService or DesiredRelease records per-node via direct etcd writes or gRPC calls outside the BOM pipeline, creating records that the promotion reconciler did not validate.
Manually run systemctl start globular-mcp.service after a Day-1 install to work around a missing start_services step in the spec, without updating the spec.
Mark a release wave as AVAILABLE immediately after a single node reports local install success, before all wave participants have confirmed and before the commit RPC succeeds.
Mark a service release AVAILABLE based solely on a local node reporting success, without the controller confirming installed-state in etcd.
Mark a ServiceRelease as AVAILABLE based on a node-local install success message without waiting for the wave-level commit to succeed in the workflow service.
mcp spec missing start_services step — Day-1 install does not start mcp
Orphaned mcp_server process holds port 10260, blocking service start
MinIO binary version differs across nodes causing auth failures
Cluster identity, TLS SAN validation, and service routing must not be bypassed with localhost or 127.0.0.1
Node installs wrong version from stale reconciler after leader failover
Objectstore desired state must be registry-governed
Node-agent infers MinIO membership from local disk
Objectstore desired state key has no registry governance
Missing inventory in a partial objectstore snapshot must be treated as unknown, not known-down
MinIO topology contract
Publish /globular/objectstore/config without adding it to config.CriticalEtcdKeys and critical_state_registry, leaving objectstore desired state without governance.
Publish /globular/pki/ca without adding it to config.CriticalEtcdKeys and critical_state_registry, leaving CA metadata without owner, restore, or delete policy.
VIP ownership must be proven from runtime evidence, not assumed from topology or package expectations
An AI Memory entry with provenance.source=seed has content that no longer matches the awareness bundle's manifest hash
Operational-knowledge seed entries in AI Memory must match the SHA256 stamped in the awareness bundle manifest
AI Memory has zero seed-source entries even though the awareness bundle is installed and verified
A non-seeder principal attempted (and was allowed) to UPDATE or DELETE an AI Memory entry tagged provenance.source=seed
Silently overwrite a desired build_id in etcd after it has already been resolved and written, breaking audit trails and potentially installing an unverified artifact.
Write invalid or unvalidated state to the LKG file, overwriting the last valid configuration. LKG writes must pass checksum and generation validation before committing.
A local package override in desired state must carry explicit override metadata
Local/dev/hotfix builds must use a non-official publisher and a version with a local suffix
The official BOM must not be mutated when a local package override is active
Once an official stable (publisher, name, version, platform) is published, no different byte stream may claim that identity
Promoting a local fix to official must go through the official build/release pipeline
A local artifact imported from another cluster must not override this cluster's official desired state
Infer that a destructive action is desired from indirect signals (missing key, timeout, local disk state) rather than requiring explicit audited intent.
PKI CA metadata must be published and registry-governed
PKI CA metadata not published to etcd

Promote a weight change from a proposal into path_weights.yaml and then
immediately rely on the new rankings without rebuilding the graph. The graph
reads weights at build time; live scoring uses the compiled graph, not the
YAML directly.

Compute DNS records from Profile membership only — every node
with profile=gateway gets an entry in gateway.<domain> regardless
of whether the package is installed or the unit is running.
Clients hit dead endpoints on every TTL flip; new nodes appear in
DNS before they can serve traffic; nodes whose service crashed
silently stay in records until the next reconcile.

Use only n.GetIdentity().GetAdvertiseIp() and treat empty as "node has
no usable IP". Verified live 2026-05-10: the controller does not populate
AdvertiseIp on any node, so this path is always-empty and produces
false-positives (node not in pool → write_quorum_lost) or false-negatives
(skip check → real violations missed).
Reconcile lanes must not starve completion
Blocked reconcile phase starves completion and critical publishers

After acquiring cluster leadership, load desired state from an in-memory snapshot
carried over from a prior leader session rather than reading fresh from etcd.
Apply a topology change in the drift reconciler without first running topology safety preflight checks (quorum viability, participant validity, objectstore consistency).
DNS recovery requires working DNS — circular dependency
Recovery paths must not require DNS
Reduce the number of bind retries in the MCP server to avoid seeing the 'address already in use' error, without fixing the orphaned process that holds the port.

When upstream mirror lookups fail with "require_checksum=true but
request has no sha256," the operator flips require_checksum off
in repository config to "make installs proceed." The cluster now
installs whatever bytes the mirror returns without verifying they
match the desired identity. This is the canonical wrong fix for
the build_id orphaning cascade — the missing manifest, not the
enforced checksum, is the actual bug.
ServiceRelease stays FAILED after wave succeeds when workflow commit RPC fails

The DNS service itself is unhealthy on some nodes, so the operator
"fixes" it by editing dns.<domain> records to remove the bad
nodes. Now the DNS reconciler writes its own remediation back on
the next cycle because the records are computed from cluster
state, not from operator overrides. The records flap; clients see
both states in the same TTL window.

Use the awareness graph's decision paths, coverage, or blind_spots as authoritative
guidance immediately after a knowledge YAML edit, without rebuilding the graph.
A stale graph silently omits the newly added forbidden_fixes and invariants.
Restart a service unit via systemctl or Restart=on-failure without first killing any orphaned process that escaped its cgroup and may still be holding the port.

Remove EdgeForbids, EdgeTestedBy, EdgeRequiresTest, or EdgeEnforces from the
impactEdgeKinds set in impact_path.go. These are the ONLY edges that create
decision-domain traversal paths. Removing them causes decision_context to return
only information paths for all queries.
Artifact installability requires publish_state AND artifact_state to both equal PUBLISHED
Every active desired build_id MUST resolve to an installable repository artifact
package fetch returns NotFound for build_id referenced by desired state
Fallback sources MUST never fetch without manifest+checksum proof
Repository metadata authority must not depend on blob backend
Repository and MinIO form a recovery deadlock
Repository purge / GC / delete MUST refuse a build_id referenced by active desired state
Gate authoritative desired-state publication on the success of derived or projection pipelines. Projections are consumers of authority, not prerequisites.
Require hostname resolution (DNS) to run DNS repair or recovery CLI commands. DNS recovery must work via direct IP:port fallback.

Document "restart all controllers in sequence after every objectstore
apply" as the canonical operations procedure, instead of fixing the
watcher. Acceptable only as a temporary runbook entry; ossifying it as
the design is the forbidden fix.
Require service-discovery lookup when discovery itself is part of the failure being recovered from. Recovery paths must support direct endpoint configuration.
Expect COMMAND-type packages (yt-dlp, rclone, sha256sum, globular-cli) to have a systemd unit and flag them as runtime-mismatch if no unit exists.
Allow node-agent to resolve the build_id at install time rather than using the pre-resolved build_id from DesiredService in etcd.
Restart globular-mcp.service without first killing any orphaned mcp_server process from a prior run that escaped its cgroup and is holding port 10260.
Retry a failed install action on every reconcile tick without checking whether the failure is deterministic or whether the installed state was partially committed.

resolveByBuildID returns codes.NotFound when the manifest exists
but its publish_state is YANKED/REVOKED/ARCHIVED/QUARANTINED.
The node-agent installer interprets NotFound as "build absent →
try local fallback," and installs a pinned tarball even though
the repository has explicitly demoted the build. The cluster ends
up running a build the repository said "stop on."

Return a bare NO_MATCH response from awareness.decision_context without including
the coverage map (graph, runtime, code_scan, raw_yaml) and blind_spots list.
NO_MATCH without coverage context silently implies safety, which is wrong.
Route MinIO write operations through round-robin DNS without ensuring the target node is in the active objectstore pool.

Call the seeder logic directly from the controller's startup path
(synchronously or via a goroutine) instead of scheduling it as a proper
Workflow Service workflow. Bypasses idempotency, bounded retry, and the
terminal-state contract that the 4-layer model relies on.
Execute a Scylla, etcd, or DNS query in a critical reconcile path without a context deadline or timeout. All critical-path queries must be bounded at 5-15s.
Doctor flags COMMAND packages as missing systemd units
Desired-enabled state must not be used as proof that a service is alive
Doctor runtime proof must match package kind
Installed-state is not runtime liveness
Critical consumers must hold last-known-good state
Last-known-good state corrupt or absent causing service interruption
Critical Scylla keyspace replication policy
Critical Scylla keyspace replication factor below policy
Every service client constructed in service code must be classified as declared/optional/background/test
Every BOM service package must have a ServiceDesiredVersion record
Desired state writes during bootstrap must bypass the mesh
platform-upgrade omits ServiceDesiredVersion records during early bootstrap
Every service unit must kill orphaned processes before starting a new instance
etcd-registered service address must be validated reachable with the correct interface before reconciliation depends on it
A service uses os.Getenv() to resolve a cluster address — bypasses etcd source-of-truth
Orphaned service process squats original port after cgroup escape, stale address in etcd blocks reconciliation
Service restart must go through singleflight gate
Service drift must age and escalate
Service drift warning stays at WARN indefinitely

Set confidence: high on a causal rule that has only been observed once or only
in a controlled test environment. High confidence rules are presented to operators
as established fact, not hypothesis.

The resolver returns "build_id not found" so the caller "helpfully"
falls back to ResolveArtifact with no build_id, picking up the
latest published build instead. The cluster silently installs the
wrong version everywhere; the pinned build_id in desired state
becomes a decorative annotation that nobody enforces.

When BOTH an env var and an etcd key are present for an endpoint, return
the env var without logging the divergence. Two nodes with identical
Globular config behave differently because one has the env var set, and
the difference is invisible until a user reports asymmetric behaviour.

Have the doctor finding bootstrap.flag_persists_post_day_1 trigger an
automatic deletion of /var/lib/globular/bootstrap.enabled so the warning
clears. Hides a security regression behind a self-healing illusion and
masks crashes in the day-1 finalize path.

When a non-seeder principal is denied mutation of a seed entry, return
the error to the caller but skip the audit log entry or downgrade it
below WARN — to avoid log noise from "expected" denials.

When the apply-watcher goroutine has died, leave queued apply_request
keys in etcd unprocessed and rely on a future controller restart to drain
them. This is the current bug — apply requests sit for hours until a
human notices and restarts the controller.

Edit high-risk awareness files (graph/domain.go, semantic/score.go, integrity/
impact_path.go, docs/awareness/knowledge/*.yaml) without first calling
awareness.preflight or awareness.decision_context to check what the edit touches.
This is editing the safety system without reading its own guardrails first.

Return an empty blind_spots list from decisionContextNoGraph() when the graph
is unavailable. An empty blind_spots list when the graph is missing implies the
tool has full coverage — the opposite of the truth.

Add a new edge kind to graph/edges.go without adding its domain classification
to graph/domain.go. Unclassified edges default to the information domain, causing
decision-relevant edges to be silently downgraded in the semantic scorer.
Remove or disable health checks in the MCP spec or unit file to avoid WaitActive timeout errors, instead of fixing the underlying reason the service does not reach active state.

Disable the seed-immutability check when a test-environment flag is set,
so integration tests can prepare seed-tagged entries without going through
a real seeder workflow. Creates a code path that exists nowhere in
production but ships in the same binary.

When ai-memory is unreachable at seeder-schedule time, log a warning and
return success so cluster bring-up continues. Hides a real outage of the
operational-knowledge store behind a healthy-looking controller.
Skip the systemd unit liveness check because the installed-state record exists, leading to false-positive health reports.
Omit one or more service packages from the release BOM, causing them to be absent from ServicesDesiredSet and therefore never installed on cluster nodes.
Skip the workflow service entirely and write AVAILABLE directly to a ServiceRelease record in etcd to unstick a stuck release.

Branch every Ips-consuming code path on "is this node the keepalived
master?" and use different selection logic for the master. Doubles the
surface area and leaks keepalived state into every consumer.
Start the globular-minio.service based on local health checks or disk state, without verifying that the node is present in ObjectStoreDesiredState.
Allow node-agent to start MinIO when /globular/objectstore/config is absent, relying on local state or stale config instead.
Start a new global reconcile phase when completion of an already-started phase is blocked. New work must wait behind completion, not starve it.
Installed state must never be inferred from repository catalog availability
Runtime health must never be inferred from desired state
Unknown, incomplete, or stale state must not be rendered or classified as healthy
Stop a runtime service when the disable spec is malformed, missing a reason, or has a stale generation — without validating explicit intent.
Stop a managed runtime service (keepalived, envoy, minio) solely because the control-plane key is absent or could not be read. Missing key must mean HOLD_LAST_KNOWN_GOOD.
Store the artifact index, manifest, or version ledger exclusively in MinIO, making metadata unavailable when blob storage degrades.

A local build (version=1.2.43+local.ryzen.1) is validated and ready
for production. Shortcut: strip the suffix so it becomes 1.2.43 and
publish as core@globular.io/stable. Avoids the official release pipeline.
This re-introduces the identity fraud the local lane was designed to prevent:
two clusters now run different bytes under the same official identity.

On 'etcdserver: mvcc: required revision has been compacted' from the
objectstore-apply watcher, `continue` the loop and keep ranging over a
channel etcd will close on the next iteration. The goroutine exits;
nothing re-spawns it.
Topology drift reconciler must check safety contracts
Topology drift reconciler applies unsafe topology change
Treat desired-state records with source=bootstrap or authoritative=false as final authoritative state without running the controller promotion pass.
Accept a spec that fails schema validation or checksum as a valid disable-intent and stop the runtime. Invalid specs must be ignored; LKG must be held.
Interpret the absence of a desired-state key as an intention to delete the corresponding runtime state. Missing key must trigger restoration, not deletion.

Treat a NO_MATCH result from awareness.decision_context as evidence that an edit
is safe for high-risk files. NO_MATCH means the graph has no paths — it does NOT
mean no risk exists. The graph may be stale, the files may be unindexed, or the
relevant invariants may not have been added yet.
The annotation validator must not interpret //globular: directives inside raw string literals as source-level awareness contracts.
Mark every failure as transient and schedule a retry regardless of whether the root cause can resolve itself without input change.
User actions in the UI must declare and respect their required RBAC permission
AI-generated UI text may decorate wording but must not invent system state
Dangerous or destructive-operation warnings must remain visible across responsive layouts and skins
UI screens showing cluster membership, leader identity, or network identity must distinguish stable node identity from floating VIP reachability
UI status indicators must surface the freshness of their data source
UI success states must be bound to backend, workflow, or runtime confirmation — not local click or dispatch state
Every visible UI state must be bound to an explicit backend, workflow, RBAC, or local authority
The annotation validator must not treat //globular: strings inside _test.go fixtures or raw test strings as production annotations.
Single-IP node identity reads cause VIP-contamination misclassification across multiple subsystems

Extend the 30-minute bootstrap-flag TTL to hours or days so the flag
doesn't get re-created during slow joins. Trades a real-time security
boundary for cosmetic convenience.

Grant the 'admin' role permission to UPDATE/DELETE entries with
provenance.source=seed, so admin-driven workflows can mutate seed
entries directly. Removes the immutability boundary that protects
operational knowledge from poisoning.

Once one service uses os.Getenv() for its address, "for consistency" make
all the others do the same. Compounds a single rule violation into a
cluster-wide drift surface.
Wipe the MinIO .minio.sys directory or reset erasure-coded pool metadata without going through an approved cluster transition workflow.
Wipe persistent state (MinIO .minio.sys, ScyllaDB data directory, etcd snapshots) without a controller-approved transition record for the correct generation.
Workflow dispatch must gate on backend health
Workflow dispatched when backend is down
Dispatching an action is not proof that the action completed successfully
workflow dispatch timeout — terminal symptom of upstream etcd or controller instability, not the root cause

Restarting the workflow service or retrying workflow steps while etcd and controller
are unstable causes immediate re-failure: the new dispatch attempt also times out
because the control plane cannot serve it. Resolve etcd → controller → then workflow.
UI or CLI success must require backend/workflow confirmation

Workflow dispatch timeouts are always a terminal symptom of control-plane instability.
The correct fix order is: etcd → controller → workflow. Fixing or restarting the
workflow service first will not unblock it — it will re-fail immediately because the
underlying etcd/controller issue is unresolved.
Write to a critical etcd key from code that is not the registered owner in critical_state_registry. Unknown writers must be rejected.
Write desired state records only through the bootstrap gRPC call at cluster init time, without ensuring the BOM pipeline runs on every subsequent upgrade.
xds or gateway stuck in infra_preparing due to package kind classification conflict
